items.title: OpenAI reveals its first AI processor: Jalapeño
items.raw_content: OpenAI has just revealed a new "intelligence processor" chip for AI servers made in partnership with Broadcom. The chip, called Jalapeño, is designed to power current and future large language models, according to an announcement on Wednesday. Jalapeño is an ASIC (Application-Specific Integrated Circuit), meaning it's designed for a specific purpose: AI inference. With AI […]

items.title: Facebook rolls out an AI companion app for creators
items.raw_content: The new app, which is currently being tested with select creators, will have Facebook's recently launched AI creator assistant built into it.
items.summary: The new app, which is currently being tested with select creators, will have Facebook's recently launched AI creator assistant built into it.

items.title: The memory chip crunch is paying off for this US company
items.raw_content: Revenue quadrupled to $41.45 billion compared with the same period a year ago. The company's profit, meanwhile, rose from $1.88 billion to an incredible $28.2 billion year-over-year.
items.summary: Revenue quadrupled to $41.45 billion compared with the same period a year ago. The company's profit, meanwhile, rose from $1.88 billion to an incredible $28.2 billion year-over-year.

items.title: The $27 million Al proxy war over Alex Bores ends in a draw
items.raw_content: The expensive, $27 million political proxy war between Anthropic and OpenAI came to a draw last night when Alex Bores, a New York state Assemblyman whose popularity surged after being targeted by a pro-AI super PAC, narrowly lost the Democratic primary to represent New York's 12th Congressional district. items.title: Figma now has AI motion graphics and shader tools
items.raw_content: Figma has revealed some new design and coding product updates at its annual Config conference that aim to help creatives "push their ideas further" and automate tedious tasks with AI. Part of this is a reimagined canvas that's now optimized for full-stack development, according to Figma, bringing teams, AI agents, tools, and materials "together in […]

items.title: AI researchers continue to leave Google for its rivals
items.raw_content: Top AI researchers Jonas Adler and Alexander Pritzel are leaving Google for Anthropic, following departures from top scientists Noam Shazeer and John Jumper.
items.summary: Top AI researchers Jonas Adler and Alexander Pritzel are leaving Google for Anthropic, following departures from top scientists Noam Shazeer and John Jumper.

items.title: Former Infosys chief has a new startup that wants to challenge the IT services world
items.raw_content: Backed by Mayfield and Aramco Ventures, Vishal Sikka’s new venture brings together veterans from SAP, Infosys, and VianAI.
items.summary: Backed by Mayfield and Aramco Ventures, Vishal Sikka’s new venture brings together veterans from SAP, Infosys, and VianAI.

items.title: Cerebras stock plunges after earnings as CEO says margin outlook was misunderstood
items.raw_content: In its first earnings report since going public, the AI chipmaker forecast a narrower gross margin in its core business, scaring investors.
items.summary: In its first earnings report since going public, the AI chipmaker forecast a narrower gross margin in its core business, scaring investors.

items.title: AI was supposed to kill engineering jobs, but new data suggests they’re the most resilient
items.raw_content: While AI dominates the layoff narrative, engineers are actually making up a larger share of new hires, according to SignalFire data.
items.summary: While AI dominates the layoff narrative, engineers are actually making up a larger share of new hires, according to SignalFire data.

items.title: Companies are scrambling to stop employees from maxing out AI budgets with small tasks
items.raw_content: The tokenmaxxing era was brief. We now appear to be entering the era of token rationing.
items.summary: The tokenmaxxing era was brief. We now appear to be entering the era of token rationing.

items.title: Agility Robotics plans to go public via SPAC in a $2.5B deal
items.raw_content: Agility Robotics, the humanoid robotics startup that spun out of Oregon State University in 2015, expects to generate $620 million in proceeds.
items.summary: Agility Robotics, the humanoid robotics startup that spun out of Oregon State University in 2015, expects to generate $620 million in proceeds.

items.title: Figma adds code layers, support for animations, more AI features in new update
items.raw_content: Figma's update adds a new code layer, support for motion and shaders, and the ability to create custom plug-ins for various tasks using AI.
items.summary: Figma's update adds a new code layer, support for motion and shaders, and the ability to create custom plug-ins for various tasks using AI.

items.title: Congresswoman denies staff used AI to write defense funding amendment
items.raw_content: Rep. Anna Paulina Luna (R-FL) says her staff used AI for "spellcheck" in an amendment summary for a major defense bill, but denies it was used for the bill text itself and says "NO Legislation is ever drafted with AI." Luna issued the response after accounts on X began sharing screenshots of an amendment summary […]

items.title: The Google Home Speaker sounds good and looks great — but it’s finicky

items.title: Europe is pushing back on Washington’s chip war
items.raw_content: As ASML CEO Christophe Fouquet told TechCrunch in May, what China can currently buy are older-generation deep ultraviolet tools — gear first shipped about a decade ago — the same machines the MATCH Act would now put off-limits.

items.title: Something’s off with Midjourney’s pivot to body scanners
items.raw_content: Last week, Midjourney, an AI startup best known for its image generator, made an unusual pivot: medical imaging. items.title: Google Home will soon get better at recognizing you
items.raw_content: A new update for Google Home could make it less likely your smart home cameras mistake you for someone else, just because you're facing away from the camera.
